headline: SanDisk (SNDK) Stock Soars 11.6% Today: Micron Technology (MU) and Western Digital (WDC) Also See Strong Gains
summary: Monday was a strong day, to say the least, for memory and storage stocks. headline: Bank of America’s Funding Shift From Floating to Fixed: Subtle Clue About BAC’s Risk Strategy?
summary: In early March 2026, Bank of America Corporation issued several new senior unsecured fixed and zero-coupon notes across 2029–2046 maturities while simultaneously redeeming existing floating-rate senior notes in US dollars, euros, and Japanese yen. This rapid shift from floating to fixed-rate funding, paired with callable medium-term note issuance, highlights how the bank is actively reshaping its liability mix and interest-rate exposure. We’ll now examine how this refinancing activity,...

headline: U.S. Art Market Rebounds, Posting a 23% Increase in Auction Sales
summary: The U.S. art market regained momentum in 2025, with total auction sales rising 23% year-over-year to $3.17 billion, according to Bank of America's 2026 U.S. Art Market Report in partnership with ArtTactic®. The increase represents the market's first annual growth since 2022, signaling stabilization after more than two years of contraction.

headline: Warren Buffett Just Did Something He Almost Never Does — and It’s a Signal Retirees Shouldn’t Ignore
summary: Warren Buffett has been a net seller of stocks for 13 consecutive quarters, which is equivalent to more than three straight years of selling more than he’s buying, the longest streak of its kind in his entire career. Over this stretch, Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK.B) has unloaded massive positions in Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL), Bank of America (NYSE:BAC), ... headline: K-shaped economy puts pressure on women, as lower pay and higher costs reduce spending
summary: Women are under pressure from lower pay and reduced affordability on everyday items, which is widening the K-shaped economy and seeing women spending less.

headline: How Apollo Tyres (NSEI:APOLLOTYRE) Story Is Shifting As Valuation Assumptions Are Tweaked
summary: The fair value for Apollo Tyres is held at ₹562.71 per share, so the latest update is more about fine tuning assumptions than resetting the price anchor. Analysts are drawing on recent work around capital allocators to frame how small shifts in required return and risk perception, rather than big headline target moves, can still influence how you think about the tyre maker’s equity story. Read on to see how this evolving narrative might shape the way you track Apollo Tyres from here. Analyst...
